title: BEM-921 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM921_WORKER_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_V2_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-921 WORKER SYSTEM PROTOCOL V2 REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- The updated operator decisions resolve the ambiguity in BEM-920. The new protocol must explicitly define role-level algorithms, not only architecture nouns.
- The inner contour algorithm must be: Analyst receives input, prepares proposal, Auditor approves or rejects proposal, Executor runs only approved proposal, Executor reports to Auditor, Auditor accepts result or sends revision through Analyst; the loop repeats until auditor-approved result.
- Workspace must be assigned to internal contours, not to individual elements. Main workspace is approved repository state/code.
- Curator is a role/element of object and inherits system/object/channel rules. Curator is not bound to contour rules unless explicitly assigned into a contour.
- Analyst/Auditor/Executor are typical role elements inside internal contours and inherit system/object/contour rules/properties and experience refs.
- No local element rules by default. Specialization must come from contour properties and registry refs.
- Vertical links must be between curators. Horizontal links must be Auditor A to Analyst B, or Analyst B request to Auditor A.
- GitHub Actions and runners must be described as activation/proof mechanisms and must write event log/proof records.
- Telegram must be treated as one module of the whole system and may exist for registered curators. The main top-level operator route remains GD Curator.
- Claude requirements remain mandatory and must be mapped to BEM-920..BEM-928 acceptance criteria.
Required prompt drafts:
- Object prompts: General Director, Director, Worker.
- Role templates: Curator, Analyst, Auditor, Executor.
- Contour prompt overlays: GD strategy contour, GD system-rules contour, DIR analysis/planning contour, DIR worker-rules contour, WRK dynamic execution contour, WRK testing contour.
- Channel/runner prompts or operational contracts: Managed channel consumer, dispatch consumer, GitHub Actions proof runner, Telegram input handler.
Approval note:
- These prompt drafts can be approved as templates. Final generated prompts should reference registries, not duplicate all rules.
Recommended status:
- SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_V2_APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS.
No issue comments.
